content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                    EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1 K Street
Sacramento 95814
(916) 322-5660

November 26, 1975


Vigo G. Nielsen
Dobbs, Doyle & Nielsen
Suite 2500, The Alcoa Building
One Maritime Plaza
San Francisco 94111

                                          Re: Request for Opinion
                                              No. 75-200

Dear Mr. Nielsen:

     Thank you for your letter of November 7, 1975,
requesting an opinion with regard to reporting require-
ments for a lobbyist who is an employee of a lobbyist
firm. Since your letter raises no substantial questions
involving interpretation of the Political Reform Act, no
formal opinion will be issued. However, I hope the fol-
lowing informal comments will be helpful.

     2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18619(c) requires a
lobbyist who is employed by a lobbyist firm to list
both his immediate employer and the employer's clients
"as his or her employers" on the Lobbyist Registration
Statement (Form 61). On each Lobbyist Report (from 620),
the lobbyist must report the name of each client for
whom he has engaged in any lobbying activity during the
period, as well as the lobbyist firm that employs him.
The lobbyist need not prorate his salary among the clients
he represents. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18619(e). Both
the lobbyist firm and the firm's clients should be listed
on the Form 620 under "Name and Address of Employer or
Client." After consideration of your suggestions, the
staff has concluded that this scheme serves a legitimate
purpose and, thus, the staff does not intend to seek an
amendment to this regulation.

     The firm, corporation or other business entity
which employs a lobbyist firm must file lobbyist employer
reports pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18619(a).
However, these reports are required to show the amounts

Vigo G. Nielsen
November 26, 1975
Page Two


paid to a lobbyist firm as "payments to influence legislative or administrative action," not as salary payments
to a lobbyist. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 13619(d).

If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion
request, you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 13321, a copy of which is attached hereto.

If you have any questions, please contact Natalie
E. West, an attorney on the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman, for the Commission

DHL:jp

DOBBS, DOYLE & NIELSEN
Attorneys and Counselors at Law

SUITE 2560 THE ALCOA BUILDING
ONE MARITIME PLAZA
SAN FRANCISCO CA 94111

TELEPHONE 415 362-1560

November 7, 1975


Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re:    Opinion Request Concerning
       2 Cal Admin Code § 18619

Dear Commissioners:

     After lengthy discussions with the Secretary
of State's office and with Mr. Nothenberg of your
staff, we request clarification on the reporting
requirements for a lobbyist who is an employee of a
lobbyist firm pursuant to 2 Cal Admin § 18619.

     According to subsection (c) of this regulation,
an employee of the retained entity who also qualifies
as a lobbyist shall list on his or her registration not
only the name of his or her direct and immediate employer
but the name or names of the person or persons who have
retained his or her immediate employer for said lobbying
as additional employers. The actual client shall not only
authorize the lobbyist firm but authorize the individual
employee to do such lobbying.

     As the regulation is presently stated and as
interpreted by the Secretary of State's office, it
appears to require that the actual client not only
authorize the employee of the firm but that the client
list the employee on every monthly report. In the true
employee situation, this would indicate no compensation
or expenses to the employee, since all compensation is
to the employer. Also, the employee would list all clients
of the employer, for which he or she does some lobbying, on
each monthly report again indicating no compensation from
such clients. Such information would appear to serve no
useful purpose and, in fact, would distort the facts by
showing no compensation from a specific list of clients

Fair Political Practices Commission
November 7, 1975
Page two


for which lobbyiing is being performed. Since the
employer would not prorate compensation from the
client to the employee's salary, there is no real
pass through and no way to provide a client infor-
mation on how much of his or her fee is used for
salary of the employee-lobbyist.

It is understood that the immediate employer
will file either a Form 650 with respect to its employee,
or if the immediate employer is itself a lobbyist will
either file a Form 650 or will attach the same infor-
mation as an attachment to its Form 620/625 pursuant to
Regulation 18619(e). Thus, will be disclosed all com-
pensation and expenses paid by the employer to the
employee. And the client will file Form 650 disclosing
fees paid the employer.

In those cases in which the employee will only
receive compensation from his or her direct and imme-
diate employer, would it not be advisable that Regulation
18619(c) be amended or clarified to not require the
listing of clients of the employee's employer on the
employee's registration as employers of the employee,
but require the employee to list the names and addresses
of those clients of his employer for whom he does lobby-
ing as additional information to be included with his
registration, without making such persons his "employers."

Very truly yours,

Viggo G. Nielsen, Jr.

VGN:jy


This Opinion Request is being asked on behalf
of C. W. (Nick) Medeiros, a registered lobbyist.

However, I hope the following informal comments will be helpful. 2 Cal. Adm. On each Lobbyist Report (Form 620), the lobbyist must report the name of each client for whom he has engaged in any lobbying activity during the period, as well as the lobbyist firm that employs him. The lobbyist need not prorate his salary among the clients he represents. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18619(e). Both the lobbyist firm and the firm's clients should be listed on the Form 620 under "Name and Address of Employer or Client."
